column4: And {{c1::Jacob}} lived in the land of Egypt seventeen years; so the days of Jacob, the years of his life, were a hundred forty and seven years.
And Jacob lived in the land of {{c1::Egypt}} seventeen years; so the days of Jacob, the years of his life, were a hundred forty and seven years.

And the time drew near that {{c1::Israel}} must die; and he called his son Joseph, and said unto him: ‘If now I have found favour in thy sight, put, I pray thee, thy hand under my thigh, and deal kindly and truly with me; bury me not, I pray thee, in Egypt.

And the time drew near that Israel must die; and he called his son {{c1::Joseph}}, and said unto him: ‘If now I have found favour in thy sight, put, I pray thee, thy hand under my thigh, and deal kindly and truly with me; bury me not, I pray thee, in Egypt.
But when {{c1::I}} sleep with my fathers, thou shalt carry me out of Egypt, and bury me in their burying-place.’ And he said: ‘I will do as thou hast said.’
But when I sleep with my fathers, thou shalt carry me out of {{c1::Egypt}}, and bury me in their burying-place.’ And he said: ‘I will do as thou hast said.’
And he said: ‘{{c1::Swear}} unto me.’ And he swore unto him. And Israel bowed down upon the bed’s head.
And he said: ‘Swear unto me.’ And he swore unto him. And {{c1::Israel}} bowed down upon the bed’s head.
And it came to pass after these things, that one said to {{c1::Joseph}}: ‘Behold, thy father is sick.’ And he took with him his two sons, Manasseh and Ephraim.
And it came to pass after these things, that one said to Joseph: ‘{{c1::Behold}}, thy father is sick.’ And he took with him his two sons, Manasseh and Ephraim.
And one told {{c1::Jacob}}, and said: ‘Behold, thy son Joseph cometh unto thee.’ And Israel strengthened himself, and sat upon the bed.
And one told Jacob, and said: ‘{{c1::Behold}}, thy son Joseph cometh unto thee.’ And Israel strengthened himself, and sat upon the bed.
And {{c1::Jacob}} said unto Joseph: ‘God Almighty appeared unto me at Luz in the land of Canaan, and blessed me,
And Jacob said unto {{c1::Joseph}}: ‘God Almighty appeared unto me at Luz in the land of Canaan, and blessed me,

and said unto me: {{c1::Behold}}, I will make thee fruitful, and multiply thee, and I will make of thee a company of peoples; and will give this land to thy seed after thee for an everlasting possession.

and said unto me: Behold, {{c1::I}} will make thee fruitful, and multiply thee, and I will make of thee a company of peoples; and will give this land to thy seed after thee for an everlasting possession.
And now thy two sons, who were born unto thee in the land of {{c1::Egypt}} before I came unto thee into Egypt, are mine; Ephraim and Manasseh, even as Reuben and Simeon, shall be mine.
And now thy two sons, who were born unto thee in the land of Egypt before {{c1::I}} came unto thee into Egypt, are mine; Ephraim and Manasseh, even as Reuben and Simeon, shall be mine.
And thy issue, that thou {{c1::begettest}} after them, shall be thine; they shall be called after the name of their brethren in their inheritance.
And thy issue, that thou begettest after them, shall be thine; they shall be {{c1::called}} after the name of their brethren in their inheritance.

And as for me, when {{c1::I}} came from Paddan, Rachel died unto me in the land of Canaan in the way, when there was still some way to come unto Ephrath; and I buried her there in the way to Ephrath—the same is Beth-lehem.’

And {{c1::Israel}} beheld Joseph’s sons, and said: ‘Who are these?’
And Israel beheld {{c1::Joseph’s}} sons, and said: ‘Who are these?’
And {{c1::Joseph}} said unto his father: ‘They are my sons, whom God hath given me here.’ And he said: ‘Bring them, I pray thee, unto me, and I will bless them.’
And Joseph said unto his father: ‘{{c1::They}} are my sons, whom God hath given me here.’ And he said: ‘Bring them, I pray thee, unto me, and I will bless them.’
Now the eyes of {{c1::Israel}} were dim for age, so that he could not see. And he brought them near unto him; and he kissed them, and embraced them.
Now the eyes of Israel were dim for age, so that he could not see. And he {{c1::brought}} them near unto him; and he kissed them, and embraced them.
And {{c1::Israel}} said unto Joseph: ‘I had not thought to see thy face; and, lo, God hath let me see thy seed also.’
And Israel said unto {{c1::Joseph}}: ‘I had not thought to see thy face; and, lo, God hath let me see thy seed also.’
And {{c1::Joseph}} brought them out from between his knees; and he fell down on his face to the earth.

And {{c1::Joseph}} took them both, Ephraim in his right hand toward Israel’s left hand, and Manasseh in his left hand toward Israel’s right hand, and brought them near unto him.
And Joseph took them both, {{c1::Ephraim}} in his right hand toward Israel’s left hand, and Manasseh in his left hand toward Israel’s right hand, and brought them near unto him.

And {{c1::Israel}} stretched out his right hand, and laid it upon Ephraim’s head, who was the younger, and his left hand upon Manasseh’s head, guiding his hands wittingly; for Manasseh was the first-born.

And he blessed {{c1::Joseph}}, and said: ‘The God before whom my fathers Abraham and Isaac did walk, the God who hath been my shepherd all my life long unto this day,
And he blessed Joseph, and said: ‘The God before whom my fathers {{c1::Abraham}} and Isaac did walk, the God who hath been my shepherd all my life long unto this day,

the angel who hath redeemed me from all evil, bless the lads; and let my name be named in them, and the name of my fathers {{c1::Abraham}} and Isaac; and let them grow into a multitude in the midst of the earth.’

the angel who hath redeemed me from all evil, bless the lads; and let my name be named in them, and the name of my fathers Abraham and {{c1::Isaac}}; and let them grow into a multitude in the midst of the earth.’

And when {{c1::Joseph}} saw that his father was laying his right hand upon the head of Ephraim, it displeased him, and he held up his father’s hand, to remove it from Ephraim’s head unto Manasseh’s head.

And when Joseph saw that his father was laying his right hand upon the head of {{c1::Ephraim}}, it displeased him, and he held up his father’s hand, to remove it from Ephraim’s head unto Manasseh’s head.
And {{c1::Joseph}} said unto his father: ‘Not so, my father, for this is the first-born; put thy right hand upon his head.’
And Joseph said unto his {{c1::father}}: ‘Not so, my father, for this is the first-born; put thy right hand upon his head.’

And his father refused, and said: ‘{{c1::I}} know it, my son, I know it; he also shall become a people, and he also shall be great; howbeit his younger brother shall be greater than he, and his seed shall become a multitude of nations.’

And his {{c1::father}} refused, and said: ‘I know it, my son, I know it; he also shall become a people, and he also shall be great; howbeit his younger brother shall be greater than he, and his seed shall become a multitude of nations.’
And he blessed them that day, saying: ‘By thee shall {{c1::Israel}} bless, saying: God make thee as Ephraim and as Manasseh.’ And he set Ephraim before Manasseh.
And he blessed them that day, saying: ‘By thee shall Israel bless, saying: God make thee as {{c1::Ephraim}} and as Manasseh.’ And he set Ephraim before Manasseh.
And {{c1::Israel}} said unto Joseph: ‘Behold, I die; but God will be with you, and bring you back unto the land of your fathers.
And Israel said unto {{c1::Joseph}}: ‘Behold, I die; but God will be with you, and bring you back unto the land of your fathers.
Moreover {{c1::I}} have given to thee one aportion above thy brethren, which I took out of the hand of the Amorite with my sword and with my bow.’

And {{c1::Jacob}} called unto his sons, and said: ‘Gather yourselves together, that I may tell you that which shall befall you in the end of days.
And Jacob called unto his sons, and said: ‘{{c1::Gather}} yourselves together, that I may tell you that which shall befall you in the end of days.
Assemble yourselves, and hear, ye sons of {{c1::Jacob}}; And hearken unto Israel your father.
Assemble yourselves, and hear, ye sons of Jacob; And hearken unto {{c1::Israel}} your father.
Reuben, thou art my first-born, {{c1::My}} might, and the first-fruits of my strength; The excellency of dignity, and the excellency of power.
{{c1::Reuben}}, thou art my first-born, My might, and the first-fruits of my strength; The excellency of dignity, and the excellency of power.
Unstable as water, have not thou the excellency; {{c1::Because}} thou wentest up to thy father’s bed; Then defiledst thou it—he went up to my couch.
{{c1::Unstable}} as water, have not thou the excellency; Because thou wentest up to thy father’s bed; Then defiledst thou it—he went up to my couch.
Simeon and {{c1::Levi}} are brethren; Weapons of violence their kinship.
Simeon and Levi are brethren; {{c1::Weapons}} of violence their kinship.
Let my soul not come into their council; {{c1::Unto}} their assembly let my glory not be not united; For in their anger they slew men, And in their self-will they houghed oxen.
Let my soul not come into their {{c1::council}}; Unto their assembly let my glory not be not united; For in their anger they slew men, And in their self-will they houghed oxen.
Cursed be their anger, for it was fierce, And their wrath, for it was cruel; {{c1::I}} will divide them in Jacob, And scatter them in Israel.
Cursed be their anger, for it was fierce, And their wrath, for it was cruel; I will divide them in {{c1::Jacob}}, And scatter them in Israel.
Judah, thee shall thy brethren praise; {{c1::Thy}} hand shall be on the neck of thine enemies; Thy father’s sons shall bow down before thee.
Judah, thee shall thy {{c1::brethren}} praise; Thy hand shall be on the neck of thine enemies; Thy father’s sons shall bow down before thee.
Judah is a lion’s whelp; From the prey, my son, thou art gone up. {{c1::He}} stooped down, he couched as a lion, And as a lioness; who shall rouse him up?
Judah is a {{c1::lion’s}} whelp; From the prey, my son, thou art gone up. He stooped down, he couched as a lion, And as a lioness; who shall rouse him up?
The sceptre shall not depart from {{c1::Judah}}, Nor the ruler’s staff from between his feet, As long as men come to Shiloh; And unto him shall the obedience of the peoples be.
The sceptre shall not depart from Judah, {{c1::Nor}} the ruler’s staff from between his feet, As long as men come to Shiloh; And unto him shall the obedience of the peoples be.
